feed.title: Daily Digest
feed.subtitle: Quote, fact, life hack, fortune cookie, and headlines of the day

entry.title: Daredevils reach top of Empire State Building with banner, before apparent proposal
entry.content: Daredevils reach top of Empire State Building with banner, before apparent proposal

entry.title: Cash-strapped British military embarks on radical new course
entry.content: Following London's belated publication of its long-awaited, highly controversial Defense Investment Plan, political and defense circles in Britain are in an uproar, with the prime minister and the opposition leader sparring Wednesday in fiery exchanges in Parliament.

entry.title: Tobias Harris reportedly reaches 2-year, $31 million deal to join Spurs
entry.content: Tobias Harris helped lead the Pistons to the No. 1 seed in the Eastern Conference last season.

entry.title: New Jersey Devils re-sign captain Nico Hischier for 5 years, $58.5 million
entry.content: Talks with new general manager Sunny Mehta on the future led to Nico Hischier signing up to stay for the long term.

entry.title: Pistons free agency tracker: Live signings, trades, news, updates and more in 2026 NBA offseason
entry.content: This summer will be about building on a good thing for the Pistons.

entry.title: See where Miami Hurricanes rank in College Football 27 ratings
entry.content: With cover athlete Malachi Toney and Darian Mensah leading the way, the Miami Hurricanes rank among the best in the College Football 27 video game.

entry.title: Why Greg Gard, others like Wisconsin's choice of Shawn Eichorst as AD
entry.content: Greg Gard, Kelly Sheffield, Yvette Healy and Jim Delany are among the many coaches or administrators who have spoken highly of new AD Shawn Eichorst.

entry.title: Why Greg Gard, others like Wisconsin's choice of Shawn Eichorst as AD
entry.content: Greg Gard, Kelly Sheffield, Yvette Healy and Jim Delany are among the many coaches or administrators who have spoken highly of new AD Shawn Eichorst.

entry.title: Brother of former Jaguars' DL Calais Campbell charged in mother's death
entry.content: The brother of former Jaguars' DL Calais Campbell has been charged in mother's death.

entry.title: National Test Scores Are Dropping. This State Is Fighting Back.
entry.content: Schools across the U.S. are scrambling to help struggling readers. Tennessee’s back-to-basics approach is showing promise.

entry.title: Trump urges acting spy chief to declassify records, including on 2020 election
entry.content: Trump urges acting spy chief to declassify records, including on 2020 election

entry.title: John Brennan Sues Trump Administration Over Investigation Records
entry.content: The former CIA director argues that he needs the materials to challenge any potential indictment.

entry.title: How the Evolution of American Cities Defined a Nation
entry.content: The top U.S. cities exerted their influence as centers of commerce, trade, finance and dissent.

entry.title: U.S. Naval Research Laboratory is building 'decision superiority' tools with AI
entry.content: American warfighters equipped with the latest technology increasingly find themselves grappling with an endless sea of information -- so much so that the U.S. military needs new tools to cut through and make sense of the data overload.

entry.title: Changing the Constitution: Five Proposals for a More Perfect Union
entry.content: We asked five experts across the political spectrum: What would you change about the U.S. Constitution if you could?

entry.title: Lone star ticks are covering much of the U.S. Here's what you need to know
entry.content: It's a tick that hunts you down and transmits a potentially dangerous allergy to red meat. The New Yorker writer Burkhard Bilger discusses the lone star tick and the risks of alpha-gal syndrome.

entry.title: Canada to ​join Eurovision song contest from 2027
entry.content: Country is first to join since Australia in 2015 as event director says it ‘continues to welcome the world’Canada will ​join the Eurovision song contest in 2027, becoming the first new ⁠participant since Australia in 2015, organisers have announced.Participation is not limited to countries in geographic Europe and instead is open to all members of the European Broadcasting Union (EBU), which Canada joined last week. Australia is an associate member. Continue reading...

entry.title: Lethal Weapon star Danny Glover reveals Alzheimer’s diagnosis
entry.content: The 79-year-old actor told media ‘I don’t feel like it’s the end of my life. There’s work to do. Your life continues’Lethal Weapon actor Danny Glover has revealed he has been living with Alzheimer’s for several years.Glover, 79, announced the news during an interview on The Today Show, during which he explained that he had been diagnosed “not long” after receiving an honorary Oscar in 2022. “I could live with it, in a sense. I’m sure as it advances, things are going to be different and changing,” he said, adding that his movements, speech and memory have slowed. However, the support of his family, who he said “have got my back”, was getting him through. Continue reading...

entry.title: ‘The silence was the worst part’: after culling his entire flock, US poultry farmer now fears bird flu
entry.content: As bird flu reshapes agriculture, small farms operate under strict rules. Just a single case could tank their businessJoshua Beebe often starts his day by cleaning the tires of trucks and cars entering his poultry farm.“We spray them off and scrub them with a brush. It’s a precaution; the goal is to eliminate as many potential avenues for a pathogen to enter as possible,” said the owner of Tardif Poultry Farm, located in the Connecticut countryside east of Hartford. Continue reading...

entry.title: Inflation peaked in May as energy prices fell in June, Kalshi traders think
entry.content: Speculators on the prediction market platform give less than 30% odds to inflation peaking above 4.2% in 2026.

entry.title: Author of England maternity care review ‘listened to wrong voices’, says adviser
entry.content: Dr Bill Kirkup says section of Valerie Amos’s report criticising ‘normal birth ideology’ was removed before publicationThe head of an inquiry into maternity care altered its final report to remove criticism of “normal birth ideology”, one of her expert advisers claims.Dr Bill Kirkup said Valerie Amos “listened to the wrong voices” before a section outlining the potential risks of encouraging women to have a vaginal birth “disappeared” from the final version of her government-commissioned report. Continue reading...

entry.title: British actor Michael Byrne dies at 82
entry.content: British actor Michael Byrne, known for his roles in “Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows: Part 1” and “Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade,” died June 20.

entry.title: What’s really in a hotdog? Nutrition experts explain
entry.content: Americans eat 20bn hotdogs every year, but experts say they’re also among the most highly processed foodsIn 1969, the late writer William Zinsser toured a hotdog factory and described his visit in Life magazine, opening with the lines: “I’ve often wondered what goes into a hotdog. Now I know and I wish I didn’t.”All these years later, his words still reflect our love/hate relationship with the humble wiener. We love eating them, but would rather not think about what’s in them. Continue reading...

entry.title: 'Magnificent 7' stocks' rough June pushes them into the red for the year. What's behind the decline
entry.content: The group — which Microsoft, Nvidia, Alphabet, Apple, Meta, Tesla and Amazon — shaved off roughly $2 trillion in market cap during June.

entry.title: Can creditors charge interest on unpaid debts after someone dies?
entry.content: When it comes to interest, what happens after death depends on the debt, the estate and the settlement timeline.

entry.title: Actor Danny Glover reveals Alzheimer's diagnosis
entry.content: Actor Danny Glover spoke about his Alzheimer's diagnosis in an interview with NBC's "Today" show, revealing that he has been living with the disease for several years.

entry.title: Trump lauds "very good meetings" as indirect U.S.-Iran talks resume
entry.content: Vance says the U.S. has accomplished its "core mission" in Iran, as Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner meet mediators in Qatar, but not Iranians.

entry.title: Sony to stop making physical discs for PlayStation starting in 2028
entry.content: After January 2028, new games will be available exclusively in digital format from the PlayStation Store and at retailers, Sony said.

entry.title: USMNT "ready to run through a wall" ahead of World Cup knockout stage, Charlie Davies says
entry.content: The U.S. men's national soccer team performed well in the group stage of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, but they haven't won a knockout match since 2002. "It's the pressure time," former USMNT player Charlie Davies said.

entry.title: Venezuelan police officers arrested over alleged looting after earthquakes
entry.content: Anger at authorities and government grows as local people, volunteers and rescue teams continue search for survivors Four Venezuelan police officers have been arrested and are facing dismissal after being accused of looting cash from the rubble of a building that collapsed during last week’s devastating twin earthquakes.Local people and national and international rescue teams continue to search for survivors in the aftermath of the back-to-back quakes, which have killed almost 2,000 people, injured more than 10,000, and left tens of thousands missing. Continue reading...

entry.title: Colorado's Melat Kiros poised to become first Gen Z member of Congress after primary win
entry.content: Melat Kiros, the Democratic socialist candidate who won the primary for Colorado's 1st Congressional District, could become the first Gen Z member of Congress. CBS News' Taurean Small has the latest.

entry.title: Vance says Iran war will have "win-win outcome for the American people" regardless of negotiations
entry.content: Vice President JD Vance says that the U.S. has "all the cards in the negotiation" with Iran. CBS News national security analyst Aaron MacLean weighs in.

entry.title: Texas Republican chair says Trump will galvanize voters with first-ever GOP midterm convention
entry.content: President Trump announced that Republicans will hold a midterm convention in Texas as his party ramps up efforts to sway voters and maintain power through the 2026 election. Texas GOP chair D'rinda Randall joins CBS News with more on the event.

entry.title: Trump Takes Maiden Flight on Qatari-Gifted Air Force One
entry.content: The president is traveling to North Dakota for the opening of Theodore Roosevelt’s presidential library.

entry.title: Sudan’s RSF committed crimes against humanity in El Fasher, Amnesty says
entry.content: Report accuses paramilitary force of crimes including ethnic cleansing in systemic campaign against civiliansThe Sudanese paramilitary Rapid Support Forces committed crimes against humanity and ethnic cleansing during its campaign to capture El Fasher, Amnesty International has alleged.Many of the crimes, including murder, torture, rape, enslavement and sexual slavery, were carried out as part of a widespread and systematic attack against civilians and amounted to crimes against humanity, the human rights organisation said in a report released on Wednesday. Continue reading...

entry.title: Zelenskyy says Ukraine hits a Russian oil refinery for the second time in a week
entry.content: Zelenskyy says Ukraine hits a Russian oil refinery for the second time in a week

entry.title: Supreme Court Deals Major Blow to Trump on Birthright Citizenship
entry.content: The Supreme Court handed down another round of key rulings during the final day of its term, including striking down an effort by President Donald Trump to limit birthright citizenship. The high court handed conservatives a win by upholding state laws that bar transgender athletes from joining girls’ sports teams. NBC’s Laura Jarrett reports for TODAY.

entry.title: Watch Wimbledon: Evans' career ends with doubles defeat, Osaka into third round
entry.content: Watch coverage, listen to radio commentary and follow text updates from day three of Wimbledon 2026.

entry.title: Rangers 'fit all the criteria' for Neil after Defoe advice
entry.content: Dan Neil sought advice from Jermain Defoe before joining Rangers and believes the Ibrox club has the right "characteristics". Neil has signed a three-year with Derek McInnes' side after eight years at Sunderland and a short loan spell at Ipswich Town last season in which he helped the Tractor Boys win promotions to the Premier League. Former Sunderland striker Defoe scored 32 goals in 74 Rangers appearances, helping them win the 2020-21 Scottish Premiership.

entry.title: Where to watch England vs. DR Congo live stream, TV channel, start time for World Cup Round of 32 match
entry.content: Here's everything you need to know to watch the England vs. DR Congo World Cup match, including where to watch on TV or live stream, what time the game starts and more.

entry.title: Expansion clock is ticking. Do Big Ten, SEC make final move? | Opinion
entry.content: Big Ten and SEC have a shrinking window to expand before new federal legislation could force media rights pooling.

entry.title: Brother of Calais Campbell charged with murder in mother's death
entry.content: One of the brothers of Ravens defensive lineman Calais Campbell has been charged with murder in the death of his mother, Nateal Campbell, 71.

entry.title: Has England won the World Cup? Inside the Three Lions' all-time record at FIFA finals
entry.content: England are chasing a second FIFA World Cup title at the 2026 tournament. Here's a look at the Three Lions' World Cup record and whether Thomas Tuchel's side can end their 60-year wait for glory.

entry.title: Tottenham defender pushing for exit with Barcelona among his preferred destinations – report
entry.content: According to SPORT, Cristian Romero is eager to leave Tottenham Hotspur this summer as he looks to bring his Premier League spell to an end, with Spain emerging as his preferred destination. While Bar...

entry.title: Thierry Henry names Olise as France’s most important player
entry.content: Bayern Munich star Michael Olise took his assist tally to five at the World Cup on Tuesday, setting up two goals in France’s 3-0 win over Sweden in the round of 32.Kylian Mbappé is up to six goals ...

entry.title: Victor Willis, Village People lead singer, dies at 74
entry.content: Victor Willis, lead singer of the disco group Village People, whose hit "Y.M.C.A." became a fixture at rallies for President Trump, has died at the age of 74, his wife and the band said.

entry.title: Here is who is in the new Pac-12 as the league launches again
entry.content: The Pac 12 has officially relaunched with new member schools. Here is a guide to the teams in the new conference.

entry.title: Official | Krépin Diatta leaves AS Monaco
entry.content: Krépin Diatta (27) has left AS Monaco following the expiry of his deal with the Principality club, it was confirmed on Tuesday. Diatta joined Les Monégasques from Club Brugge back in 2021 for a �...

entry.title: Calls grow for 'swift answers' as Nagelsmann lands in Germany
entry.content: Calls are growing for a decision on the future of Germany coach Julian Nagelsmann, who touched down in Munich on Wednesday following his team's early World Cup exit. Nagelsmann has come under fire after four-time champions Germany were ousted 4-3 on penalties by Paraguay in the round of 32 on Monday, a result only marginally better than group stage exits in 2018 and 2022.

entry.title: Erling Haaland next World Cup match: Fixture schedule, start time, draw bracket as Norway face Brazil in R16
entry.content: Is Erling Haaland playing today? Norway's full World Cup schedule as the Manchester City star aims to help his side reach the knockout stages.

entry.title: WNBA’s Alyssa Thomas receiving death threats after incident with Caitlin Clark
entry.content: The fallout from the on-court incident involving WNBA stars Alyssa Thomas and Caitlin Clark continues to grow, but the conversation has now shifted well beyond basketball. According to a report from Legion Hoops on X, Thomas has been receiving death…

entry.title: 🎥 Straight from the dressing room! Norway coach sends message to Ancelotti
entry.content: For the first time in the country’s history, a Norwegian team has qualified for the round of 16 at a World Cup. Thanks to the 2–1 victory over Ivory Coast, they achieved something historic, as coa...

entry.title: Where do Commanders stand at summer break?
entry.content: The Washington Commanders made a good amount of changes this offseason, but where do they stand heading into summer break?

entry.title: Scouting DR Congo - where England can win the game
entry.content: England play DR Congo in the World Cup round of 32 on Wednesday. BBC Sport looks at what dangers the African side possess.

entry.title: JERA Launches Singapore LNG Unit to Expand Global Fuel Portfolio
entry.content: JERA Launches Singapore LNG Unit to Expand Global Fuel Portfolio

entry.title: New MBA graduates are expected to earn $5,000 less than last year's graduating class. Is the degree still worth it?
entry.content: New MBA graduates are expected to earn $5,000 less than last year's graduating class. Is the degree still worth it?

entry.title: Elevance Health (ELV) Enhances Health OS to Automate Clinical Reviews and Reduce Provider Administrative Burdens
entry.content: Elevance Health (ELV) Enhances Health OS to Automate Clinical Reviews and Reduce Provider Administrative Burdens

entry.title: Accenture (ACN), ServiceNow (NOW) Launch Joint Initiative to Modernize Enterprise Risk Management With Agentic AI
entry.content: Accenture (ACN), ServiceNow (NOW) Launch Joint Initiative to Modernize Enterprise Risk Management With Agentic AI

entry.title: American icons reflect on the ideals the U.S. was founded on
entry.content: Leaders in the arts, science and business, including Sundar Pichai, Jamie Lee Curtis, Katie Ledecky and Ken Burns shared their reflections on America.

entry.title: New disease threats follow Trump administration's health program cuts
entry.content: From screwworm to flesh-eating bacteria, mounting public health risks are emerging in the wake of deep cuts to federal health agencies and programs.

entry.title: Adults arrested after 16 children found in 'deplorable' conditions
entry.content: Authorities have arrested four adults on felony child endangerment charges after discovering 16 children in need of medical treatment in a rural Ohio home

entry.title: From the archive: Flour power: meet the bread heads baking a better loaf – podcast
entry.content: We are raiding the Guardian long read archives to bring you some classic pieces from years past, with new introductions from the authors.This week, from 2019: The days of the mass-produced pappy white British supermarket loaf may be numbered. Meet the bread heads revolutionising the way we eatBy Wendell Steavenson. Read by Lucy Scott Continue reading...

entry.title: The “Father of the Internet” is finally retiring
entry.content: Vinton Cerf, one of the creators of the protocols underlying the internet, will step down as Google's chief internet evangelist next week.

entry.title: Trump drops restrictions on Anthropic’s Mythos and Fable models
entry.content: Anthropic said it would begin restoring access to the Fable on July 1.

entry.title: Wayve launches $85M employee tender offer at $8.5B valuation
entry.content: Wayve’s offering is part of a growing trend of AI startups using employee tenders as a strategic tool to attract and retain talent.

entry.title: OpenAI film 'Artificial,' dropped by Amazon, finds a new home with Neon
entry.content: “Artificial,” Luca Guadagnino’s starry film about Sam Altman and OpenAI, has been acquired by the indie distributor Neon after it was dropped by Amazon MGM Studios

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
A conclusion is simply the place where you got tired of thinking.
Lucky Numbers: 48, 82, 17, 1, 40, 73

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: Use BEES WAX to waterproof your shoes.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: The glue on Israeli postage is certified kosher.

entry.title: Quote of the Day — George Bernard Shaw
entry.content: “People who say it cannot be done should not interrupt those who are doing it.” — George Bernard Shaw

entry.title: Trump’s financial disclosure lists $1.4 billion in crypto earnings, powered largely by meme coins
entry.content: The annual report also included $80 million in income from settlements tied to lawsuits he filed against companies like ABC, CBS, Meta and YouTube.

entry.title: New details in deadly Florida alligator attack
entry.content: New details in deadly Florida alligator attack

entry.title: UK poll highlights fears about access to emergency contraception
entry.content: Doctors say survey shows need for morning-after pill to be available at corner shops, petrol stations and supermarketsAlmost half of the UK population believe it would be difficult to access emergency contraception on a Sunday, while nearly two-thirds think they would struggle after 10pm, according to a survey.The research, carried out by YouGov, found that only 7% of people believe it would be difficult to access emergency contraception during the daytime on a weekday. Continue reading...

entry.title: Women with irregular periods should be checked for PMOS, NHS says
entry.content: Polyendocrine metabolic ovarian syndrome is underdiagnosed and inconsistenly managed, according to NiceUp to 4 million women with irregular periods should be investigated for polyendocrine metabolic ovarian syndrome, according to new NHS guidance.PMOS, previously known as polycystic ovarian syndrome, is believed to affect up to 13% of reproductive age women, the World Health Organization estimates. Continue reading...

entry.title: Startup Battlefield Australia application closes in days: Apply before July 6
entry.content: What if one pitch changed everything? The next company nobody has heard of yet is building something that will matter. It could be yours.

entry.title: Supreme Court rejects Trump’s push to end birthright citizenship
entry.content: President Trump is slamming a Supreme Court decision that blocked his attempt to end automatic citizenship for the children of undocumented immigrants born in the U.S. NBC News’ Laura Jarrett reports.

entry.title: Trump Made More Than $1 Billion on Crypto Deals, Part of 2025 Windfall
entry.content: The president also brought in vast sums from licensing and legal settlements, according to his latest financial disclosure report.

entry.title: Disneyland to offer $59 evening tickets next month
entry.content: Disneyland Resort is commemorating its 70th anniversary through Aug. 9, and has introduced new shows and additions to rides as part of the occasion.

entry.title: Wall Street’s Best Quarter in Six Years Will Be a Hard Act to Follow
entry.content: The Dow industrials closed June at a record high. The S&P 500 and the Nasdaq composite rose 15% and 21%, respectively, in the second quarter.

entry.title: Satellite TV Provider Dish DBS Files for Bankruptcy Following AT&T Deal Snag
entry.content: The EchoStar pay-TV subsidiary said it would repay debt once the $20 billion AT&T spectrum deal closes.

entry.title: OpenClaw is finally available on Android and iOS
entry.content: The free open source agentic program is finally invading your phone.

entry.title: He thought old-school Hollywood silent films were missing one thing — Minions
entry.content: French animator Pierre Coffin created the Minions and still voices them. For the new movie, he wanted to pay homage to Hollywood's silent clowns.

entry.title: Anthropic's new Sonnet 5 model is better at the tasks that are running up enterprise bills
entry.content: Anthropic trained its newest Sonnet model to excel at agentic tasks, which have been causing a headache for the company's enterprise customers and power users.

entry.title: France vs. Sweden live score, result, highlights from 2026 World Cup knockout game
entry.content: Free-scoring France face Sweden in a mouthwatering Round of 32 clash at the World Cup.

entry.title: Xbox layoff plans reportedly include closing Arkane, canceling Blade
entry.content: The cuts will apparently start next week.

entry.title: Serena Williams vs. Maya Joint live score, result and highlights as Wimbledon 2026 comeback ends in defeat
entry.content: The Sporting News was tracking live score updates and highlights from Serena Williams' first match at Wimbledon since 2022.

entry.title: Bulls free agency tracker: Live signings, trades, news, updates and more in 2026 NBA offseason
entry.content: Chicago has plenty of needs and plenty of cap space.

entry.title: Pistons free agency tracker: Live signings, trades, news, updates and more in 2026 NBA offseason
entry.content: This summer will be about building on a good thing for the Pistons.

entry.title: Celtics free agency tracker: Live signings, trades, news, updates and more in 2026 NBA offseason
entry.content: Boston has a lot to be figured out this offseason.

entry.title: 76ers free agency tracker: Live signings, trades, news, updates and more in 2026 NBA offseason
entry.content: Philadelphia is a team with players on different timelines.

entry.title: Clippers free agency tracker: Live signings, trades, news, updates and more in 2026 NBA offseason
entry.content: Los Angeles will have to make some decisions on key players.

entry.title: Mavericks free agency tracker: Live signings, trades, news, updates and more in 2026 NBA offseason
entry.content: Dallas is trying to build around a star player with a new coach.

entry.title: 'Zorro' is L.A.'s masked hero, but France makes him magnifique
entry.content: Jean Dujardin stars in this wonderful French take on the masked vigilante, who has been out of the hero game for 20 years and jumps back in.

entry.title: Michael Burry says he's shorting Caterpillar for the first time after it nearly doubled in the AI-driven rally of 2026
entry.content: "Caterpillar jumped out at me," Burry said. "I have never shorted Caterpillar. It has always done great for me on the long side in the past."

entry.title: The DeepMind trio who built a poker AI are now making money for quant hedge funds
entry.content: EquiLibre Technologies, a Prague-based AI lab founded by three ex-DeepMind researchers, is now valued at more than $500 million.

entry.title: Report: Marxist tycoon is funding a China-backed influence campaign against AI data centers
entry.content: A China-based American tycoon is funding a Beijing-backed influence campaign in the U.S. to block construction of large computer data centers, according to a new think tank report.

entry.title: Netflix used AI to put Gene Wilder's voice into a new reality show
entry.content: So much for magic.

entry.title: Stocks Rise, the Yen Slides
entry.content: Plus, a quiet Fed only makes investors listen harder.

entry.title: The Supreme Court will hear an appeal in the long-running case between Apple and Epic Games
entry.content: Apple filed an appeal after being found in contempt of an order related to App Store fees.

entry.title: Moses Itauma ordered to fight for IBF heavyweight title after Oleksandr Usyk gives up his belts
entry.content: Itauma and Frank Sanchez have until July 29 to reach an agreement for the bout or purse bids will be called.
